title: halimium umbellatum subsp. viscosum
intro: Halimium umbellatum subsp. viscosum, also known as Sticky Rockrose, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the Cistaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and even exhibiting antiviral properties. It can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for its medicinal use.
